query: How do I quit or de-register from my studies at the University of Vienna?

If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register for courses and exams](/en/study-organisation/).

retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

retrieved.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Application and admission period: The deadlines for your admission at the University of Vienna depend on the type of degree programme and your citizenship. You can find detailed information at the respective type of degree programme. - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of the admission: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities: It is not permitted to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register

retrieved.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
heading_path: University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
retrieved.text:
You have discontinued your studies at the VWU and want to attend courses or complete supplementary examinations there again? Returning to the VWU is possible, if you have visited the VWU for less than four semesters and the degree programme is still offered in the same form at the University of Vienna.

Detailed information regarding deadlines and costs is available [directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/). For the completion of the admission to your non-degree programme at the University of Vienna, proceed as follows:

1. Inform yourself about deadlines. Registering at the VWU is only possible within the [registration period of the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.oead.at/en/vienna).
2. First, pay the course fee at the VWU. [You can find detailed information regarding this topic directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/).
3. Wait for a few days. The University of Vienna is informed about your payment at the VWU.
4. Subsequently, the required [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is prescribed. If you do not find any payment information in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students’ Union fee), you still have to wait.
5. After paying the Students’ Union fee, wait for a few days until the prescribed fee is displayed as "received" in u:space. This may take up to seven working days. Afterwards, your student documents are available for download in u:space (Personal matters > My documents), and you are admitted to the non-degree programme again.

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
heading_path: Confirmations of admission
Info for former students
retrieved.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

retrieved.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
retrieved.text:
You were admitted to the University of Vienna in the past and want to be admitted to a non-degree programme now? Non-degree students are not admitted to a specific degree programme but, for example, to the "attending of individual courses" or to a continuing education programme.

You were admitted at the University of Vienna in the past and want to visit individual courses out of interest? If this is the case, you have to note several things:

- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme are also counted in the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively, you will be permanently excluded from the degree programme, and you cannot study this degree programme.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).
- Course places are preferably distributed among degree students.
- During a non-degree programme it is not possible to pass (partial) diploma examinations, module examinations or public defenses.

Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. Please also send us the certificate of your name change, if applicable. We will admit you to the non-degree programme and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days.

Another option is the admission to a bachelor/diploma or master programme, if you fulfil the admission requirements.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: T
retrieved.text: in Vienna and Lower Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/) (in German) Related entries: - [Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31331&cHash=a9508ca1d8a392e52ec541141e6afc7d) - Termination of a degree programme You can end your studies in the following ways: - successful graduation: You will receive your graduation documents in the StudyServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme (e.g. certificate, diploma supplement) - discontinuing your degree programme: You can print out a confirmation of withdrawal from university via u:space. Related Links: - [Closing degree programmes/withdrawal from University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Graduations](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/studienabschluesse/) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Tolerance semesters EU/EEA citizens and persons treated as equivalent only pay the Students' Union fee for the presecribed duration of study (= "minimum duration of study"). At the end of this period, they are granted two additional "tolerance semesters", during which only the Studens' Union fee is charged. If you do not complete your degree programme in the prescribed duration of studies + the two tolerance semesters, you will pay both the Students' Union fee and the tuition fee. Related Links: - [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis") Your transcript of records lists all grades, positive and negative ones. It is an official proof of your academic progress, which you need

retrieved.title: Attending courses out of interest
heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: R
retrieved.text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. Related Links: - [Re-enrolment](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Continuation of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31960&cHash=051426adf043f8ab058f4e6c4520bb53) - Re-sitting an exam It is possible to re-sit a STEOP exam three times. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa)

retrieved.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
heading_path: Admission to a non-degree programme
retrieved.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.title: Registration for courses and examinations
retrieved.text:
The following page provides instructions on how to register for courses and exams. In order to register for courses and exams, you need an active [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) as well as a valid [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

[Registration for courses](#c254735)
[Registration for exams](#c254738)

- For information about how to plan your schedule, please go to the website [Planning your semester](/en/semester-planning/).
- If you have any questions about the registration (registration periods, limited numbers of study places), please contact your [studies service center (SSC)/studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).
- The [ABC of terminology](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1) provides explanations on the university terminology.

[### Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-non-continuous-assessment/)

[### Registration for courses with continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-continuous-assessment/)

retrieved.title: Attending courses out of interest
retrieved.text:
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

retrieved.title: Studying a non-degree programme at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
You can be admitted to non-degree programmes for different reasons. In a non-degree programme

- ... you are not admitted to a specific degree programme.
- ... you can download your student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment in u:space, as soon as your [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) is received by the University of Vienna.
- ... a transcript of records will be issued for any exams you take. It is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
- ... you have to take note of several consequences.

Choose the reasons, why you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme. You will find info on the admission procedure and possible consequences you have to keep in mind as a non-degree student.

[### Attending the VWU](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/)

Receiving the Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate late (only available in German).

[### Attending courses out of interest](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/attending-courses-out-of-interest/)

[### No secondary-school leaving certificate](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/)

"Studienberechtigungsprüfung" (only available in German).

[### Making up for "subject-specific" supplementary examinations](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/making-up-for-subject-specific-supplementary-examinations-from-the-bachelor-programme/)

[### Making up for qualitative admission requirements (QZB)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/making-up-for-qualitative-admission-requirements/)

retrieved.title: Returning to the University of Vienna – Resumption of studies
retrieved.text:
You did study at the University of Vienna in the past and want to start studying again? Your steps in the admission procedure vary, depending on whether you want to be admitted to the same or a different degree programme or rather a non-degree programme.

[## I want to study the same degree programme again.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/same-degree-programme/)

[## I want to study a different degree programme.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/other-degree-programme/)

[## I want to be admitted to a non-degree programme or attend the VWU again.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/non-degree-programme/)

- [Detailed information on the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
retrieved.text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

retrieved.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
heading_path: General information about the resumption of studies
retrieved.text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. After you have resumed your studies, you can see if you still have to complete the minimum academic achievement in u:space. Make sure to check how many semesters you have left to complete the minimum academic achievement. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Curriculum: You will be admitted to the current curriculum without any exception. If the curriculum has changed, you have to submit an application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) (as you did when you first applied to the degree programme. We therefore recommend: Submit your application for the resumption of studies as early as possible! - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of resumption: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities:

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

reranked.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
reranked.text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Application and admission period: The deadlines for your admission at the University of Vienna depend on the type of degree programme and your citizenship. You can find detailed information at the respective type of degree programme. - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of the admission: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities: It is not permitted to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
heading_path: University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
reranked.text:
You have discontinued your studies at the VWU and want to attend courses or complete supplementary examinations there again? Returning to the VWU is possible, if you have visited the VWU for less than four semesters and the degree programme is still offered in the same form at the University of Vienna.

Detailed information regarding deadlines and costs is available [directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/). For the completion of the admission to your non-degree programme at the University of Vienna, proceed as follows:

1. Inform yourself about deadlines. Registering at the VWU is only possible within the [registration period of the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.oead.at/en/vienna).
2. First, pay the course fee at the VWU. [You can find detailed information regarding this topic directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/).
3. Wait for a few days. The University of Vienna is informed about your payment at the VWU.
4. Subsequently, the required [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is prescribed. If you do not find any payment information in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students’ Union fee), you still have to wait.
5. After paying the Students’ Union fee, wait for a few days until the prescribed fee is displayed as "received" in u:space. This may take up to seven working days. Afterwards, your student documents are available for download in u:space (Personal matters > My documents), and you are admitted to the non-degree programme again.

reranked.title: Confirmations for (former) students
heading_path: Confirmations of admission
Info for former students
reranked.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
reranked.text:
You were admitted to the University of Vienna in the past and want to be admitted to a non-degree programme now? Non-degree students are not admitted to a specific degree programme but, for example, to the "attending of individual courses" or to a continuing education programme.

You were admitted at the University of Vienna in the past and want to visit individual courses out of interest? If this is the case, you have to note several things:

- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme are also counted in the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively, you will be permanently excluded from the degree programme, and you cannot study this degree programme.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).
- Course places are preferably distributed among degree students.
- During a non-degree programme it is not possible to pass (partial) diploma examinations, module examinations or public defenses.

Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. Please also send us the certificate of your name change, if applicable. We will admit you to the non-degree programme and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days.

Another option is the admission to a bachelor/diploma or master programme, if you fulfil the admission requirements.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: T
reranked.text: in Vienna and Lower Austria](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/) (in German) Related entries: - [Teacher Education for General Secondary Education in Vienna and Lower Austria](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31331&cHash=a9508ca1d8a392e52ec541141e6afc7d) - Termination of a degree programme You can end your studies in the following ways: - successful graduation: You will receive your graduation documents in the StudyServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme (e.g. certificate, diploma supplement) - discontinuing your degree programme: You can print out a confirmation of withdrawal from university via u:space. Related Links: - [Closing degree programmes/withdrawal from University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - [Graduations](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/studienabschluesse/) Related entries: - [Completion of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30172&cHash=3f411d6175185cfa1b401855807771dd) - [Closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31896&cHash=2e24f8e1dcdf22ae4e1123db52ef795d) - Tolerance semesters EU/EEA citizens and persons treated as equivalent only pay the Students' Union fee for the presecribed duration of study (= "minimum duration of study"). At the end of this period, they are granted two additional "tolerance semesters", during which only the Studens' Union fee is charged. If you do not complete your degree programme in the prescribed duration of studies + the two tolerance semesters, you will pay both the Students' Union fee and the tuition fee. Related Links: - [Tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - Transcript of records ("Sammelzeugnis") Your transcript of records lists all grades, positive and negative ones. It is an official proof of your academic progress, which you need

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
reranked.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: R
reranked.text: / - Re-enrolment In order to continue your studies at the University of Vienna, you must pay your tuition fee/Students' Union (ÖH) fee in due time. If you deliberately do not pay the fee (because you do not want to continue your studies) or if you forget to pay the fee, you lose your admission to studies. In case you have paid the fee at a different Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna. You can do that until the end of the admission period. Related Links: - [Re-enrolment](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Payment of the tuition fee/Students’ Union (ÖH) fee](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Students’ Union fee (ÖH fee)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31205&cHash=ed830f954f65ec47794ec4809327b5d1) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Continuation of studies](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31960&cHash=051426adf043f8ab058f4e6c4520bb53) - Re-sitting an exam It is possible to re-sit a STEOP exam three times. If your fourth attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the exam. You can take exams that are not part of the STEOP four times. The last attempt takes place before an examination committee. If the last attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires. At the University of Vienna, re-admission to the same degree programme and other degree programmes that require you to complete the exam that you failes four times is not possible. Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa)

reranked.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
heading_path: Admission to a non-degree programme
reranked.text:
1. Register in u:space
   If this is your first time studying at the University of Vienna: Activate your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/) within the application period. Make sure you remember the login data, because you will further need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space, complete the registration.
2. Apply for admission to a non-degree programme
   Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/61/create/1644) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
3. Wait for an answer
   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

reranked.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
reranked.text: You were admitted to a regular bachelor/diploma or master programme at the University of Vienna and want to resume studying the same degree programme? Here you can find detailed information about this topic.

reranked.title: Registration for courses and examinations
reranked.text:
The following page provides instructions on how to register for courses and exams. In order to register for courses and exams, you need an active [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) as well as a valid [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

[Registration for courses](#c254735)
[Registration for exams](#c254738)

- For information about how to plan your schedule, please go to the website [Planning your semester](/en/semester-planning/).
- If you have any questions about the registration (registration periods, limited numbers of study places), please contact your [studies service center (SSC)/studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).
- The [ABC of terminology](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1) provides explanations on the university terminology.

[### Registration for courses with non-continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-non-continuous-assessment/)

[### Registration for courses with continuous assessment](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/registration-for-courses-with-continuous-assessment/)

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
reranked.text:
You are interested in individual courses at the University of Vienna and want to further your education without completing a degree programme? Then a non-degree programme is the right choice.

The information on this website applies to persons who are admitted at the University of Vienna for the first time. If you were admitted to a degree or non-degree programme at the University of Vienna in the past, please read the information on the [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/).

You need the following document as JPEG or PNG file:

- your valid passport or personal ID card (front and back)

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
You can be admitted to non-degree programmes for different reasons. In a non-degree programme

- ... you are not admitted to a specific degree programme.
- ... you can download your student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment in u:space, as soon as your [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) is received by the University of Vienna.
- ... a transcript of records will be issued for any exams you take. It is available in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
- ... you have to take note of several consequences.

Choose the reasons, why you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme. You will find info on the admission procedure and possible consequences you have to keep in mind as a non-degree student.

[### Attending the VWU](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/)

Receiving the Austrian secondary-school leaving certificate late (only available in German).

[### Attending courses out of interest](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/attending-courses-out-of-interest/)

[### No secondary-school leaving certificate](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/)

"Studienberechtigungsprüfung" (only available in German).

[### Making up for "subject-specific" supplementary examinations](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/making-up-for-subject-specific-supplementary-examinations-from-the-bachelor-programme/)

[### Making up for qualitative admission requirements (QZB)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/making-up-for-qualitative-admission-requirements/)

reranked.title: Returning to the University of Vienna – Resumption of studies
reranked.text:
You did study at the University of Vienna in the past and want to start studying again? Your steps in the admission procedure vary, depending on whether you want to be admitted to the same or a different degree programme or rather a non-degree programme.

[## I want to study the same degree programme again.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/same-degree-programme/)

[## I want to study a different degree programme.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/other-degree-programme/)

[## I want to be admitted to a non-degree programme or attend the VWU again.](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/non-degree-programme/)

- [Detailed information on the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/)

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
reranked.text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

reranked.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
heading_path: General information about the resumption of studies
reranked.text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. After you have resumed your studies, you can see if you still have to complete the minimum academic achievement in u:space. Make sure to check how many semesters you have left to complete the minimum academic achievement. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Curriculum: You will be admitted to the current curriculum without any exception. If the curriculum has changed, you have to submit an application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) (as you did when you first applied to the degree programme. We therefore recommend: Submit your application for the resumption of studies as early as possible! - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of resumption: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities:

selected.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
selected.text: universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register for courses and exams](/en/study-organisation/).

selected.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
selected.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

selected.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
selected.text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

selected.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: General information about your return to the University of Vienna
selected.text: - Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Application and admission period: The deadlines for your admission at the University of Vienna depend on the type of degree programme and your citizenship. You can find detailed information at the respective type of degree programme. - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of the admission: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities: It is not permitted to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register

selected.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
heading_path: University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
selected.text:
You have discontinued your studies at the VWU and want to attend courses or complete supplementary examinations there again? Returning to the VWU is possible, if you have visited the VWU for less than four semesters and the degree programme is still offered in the same form at the University of Vienna.

Detailed information regarding deadlines and costs is available [directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/). For the completion of the admission to your non-degree programme at the University of Vienna, proceed as follows:

1. Inform yourself about deadlines. Registering at the VWU is only possible within the [registration period of the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.oead.at/en/vienna).
2. First, pay the course fee at the VWU. [You can find detailed information regarding this topic directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/).
3. Wait for a few days. The University of Vienna is informed about your payment at the VWU.
4. Subsequently, the required [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is prescribed. If you do not find any payment information in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students’ Union fee), you still have to wait.
5. After paying the Students’ Union fee, wait for a few days until the prescribed fee is displayed as "received" in u:space. This may take up to seven working days. Afterwards, your student documents are available for download in u:space (Personal matters > My documents), and you are admitted to the non-degree programme again.

selected.title: Confirmations for (former) students
heading_path: Confirmations of admission
Info for former students
selected.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

assemble.context:
[1] Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme — General information about your return to the University of Vienna
universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register for courses and exams](/en/study-organisation/).

[2] Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

[3] Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

[4] Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme — General information about your return to the University of Vienna
- Minimum academic achievement: Since winter semester 2022/23 you have to achieve [at least 16 ECTS](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/) for each bachelor/diploma programme you are admitted to. If this is not the case, the degree programme will be closed. - Change of name: If your legal name has changed, please disclose this at an early stage. Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) and attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card and a proof of the name change (e.g. marriage certificate or official notice of the name change). - Application and admission period: The deadlines for your admission at the University of Vienna depend on the type of degree programme and your citizenship. You can find detailed information at the respective type of degree programme. - Recognition of exams: The [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) of the respective degree programme can provide information regarding recognition of prior study achievements and help you to organise your studies. - Completion of the admission: You do not have to book an online appointment. - Simultaneous admission at two Austrian universities: It is not permitted to be admitted to the same degree programme at two Austrian universities at the same time. If you have already studied the chosen degree programme at another university, please send your confirmation of withdrawal via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/). - Organising your studies: [Tips and tricks to plan your studies and register

[5] Studying a non-degree programme (again) — University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)
You have discontinued your studies at the VWU and want to attend courses or complete supplementary examinations there again? Returning to the VWU is possible, if you have visited the VWU for less than four semesters and the degree programme is still offered in the same form at the University of Vienna.

Detailed information regarding deadlines and costs is available [directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/). For the completion of the admission to your non-degree programme at the University of Vienna, proceed as follows:

1. Inform yourself about deadlines. Registering at the VWU is only possible within the [registration period of the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.oead.at/en/vienna).
2. First, pay the course fee at the VWU. [You can find detailed information regarding this topic directly at the VWU](https://vorstudienlehrgang.at/en/vienna/).
3. Wait for a few days. The University of Vienna is informed about your payment at the VWU.
4. Subsequently, the required [Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is prescribed. If you do not find any payment information in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students’ Union fee), you still have to wait.
5. After paying the Students’ Union fee, wait for a few days until the prescribed fee is displayed as "received" in u:space. This may take up to seven working days. Afterwards, your student documents are available for download in u:space (Personal matters > My documents), and you are admitted to the non-degree programme again.

[6] Confirmations for (former) students — Confirmations of admission > Info for former students
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Question: How do I quit or de-register from my studies at the University of Vienna?

generate.raw_response:
To quit or de-register from your studies at the University of Vienna, you can close your degree programme, which is possible all year round [2]. You need to: 
1. Deregister from all courses with continuous assessment and examinations via u:space [2].
2. Return any borrowed books to the University Library or institute libraries [2].
3. Message the Servicedesk to specify which degree programme you want to close for bachelor-/diploma or master programmes [2], or contact doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at for doctoral/PhD programmes [2].
Alternatively, your degree programme can also be closed if you don't pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee [3], or if you don't achieve the minimum number of credits [3].

answer:
To quit or de-register from your studies at the University of Vienna, you can close your degree programme, which is possible all year round [2]. You need to: 
1. Deregister from all courses with continuous assessment and examinations via u:space [2].
2. Return any borrowed books to the University Library or institute libraries [2].
3. Message the Servicedesk to specify which degree programme you want to close for bachelor-/diploma or master programmes [2], or contact doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at for doctoral/PhD programmes [2].
Alternatively, your degree programme can also be closed if you don't pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee [3], or if you don't achieve the minimum number of credits [3].
